diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers keep the same unsupported label and use the same reason code, so the substantive difference is not in the support decision itself. Option 2's citation to evidence-001 is somewhat more diagnostically adequate because that item directly addresses the prior low-priority status of localization and helps explain why the claim 'keep_localization_low_priority' is not established in the present context; option 1's empty citation set is permissible for an unsupported judgment but is less informative given available relevant evidence. The reason codes are identical, so there is no meaningful reason-code difference. The confidence difference appears to be calibration: option 1 assigns high confidence because no evidence directly supports the proposition as stated, while option 2 lowers confidence to medium after considering that evidence-001 partially overlaps with the topic but only supports an old state, not the current 'keep' proposition. Given the evidence bundle, the unsupported judgment seems fairly clear, so option 1's higher confidence is somewhat more adequate.
